seed_problem: Given a fixed availability/deadline trace, choose Spot/On-Demand/pause per step to minimize cost; checker replays the trace and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: generalize the single online job into N wells sharing a per-step spot-capacity trace and each carrying its own progress requirement + deadline, and add re-heat (switching) costs, turning the trivially-greedy 1-machine spot/on-demand replay into an NP-hard resource-constrained scheduling problem with time windows.
title: Deep Heat: Offline Geothermal Drilling Power Schedule
strategies: all-on-demand baseline: every well drills on-demand on steps 1..R_j (the calibration point)
greedy per-well: for each well pick its R_j cheapest steps in-window, taking spot where capacity remains, else on-demand (ignores contiguity)
global spot-slot allocation: assign the limited spot slots to the (well,step) pairs with the largest spot-vs-on-demand savings, then fill remaining requirement with on-demand
contiguity-aware local search: perturb activations to merge runs and cut warm-up costs while respecting capacity
